title: D2 generic error codes spec
description: Schema for contracts/error-codes/error-codes.spec.json — the reserved-unprefixed generic catalog the unified error-codes generator reads to emit ErrorCodes.g.cs + the constructing D2Result / D2Result<TData> semantic failure factories + per-code booleans into DcsvIo.D2.Result, and that private/tools/ts-codegen/src/error-codes-emit.ts reads to emit the @dcsv-io/d2-result catalog + factories. A domain-narrowed copy of contracts/error-codes/error-codes.canonical.schema.json — same 7-field shape, narrowed to the generic catalog's unprefixed reserved namespace (NOT_FOUND, CONFLICT, …). The generic catalog adds the partial_success category for the SOME_FOUND / PARTIAL_SUCCESS read/write-partial codes.

code.description: Wire-format error code (SCREAMING_SNAKE). Must be unique across the spec. The literal IS the wire format. The generic catalog owns the reserved unprefixed namespace; per-domain catalogs prefix every code with their domain token.

httpStatus.description: HTTP status mapping. Drives transport-side response shape. In the generic catalog each code defines its own named factory (see factoryName) — multiple codes may share a status (e.g. VALIDATION_FAILED and CANCELED are both 400 but are distinct factories), so the emitted factory is identified by factoryName + factoryShape, not status alone. Expanding the matrix requires extending this enum AND the codegen mapping.

category.description: Closed semantic/telemetry classification. NOT the factory selector — httpStatus drives factory selection. Added to the wire (gRPC trailers + JSON envelope) so consumers can do generic class-based handling (e.g. retry any infrastructure_unavailable) without importing the producer's catalog. partial_success classifies the read-partial SOME_FOUND (206) + write-partial PARTIAL_SUCCESS (207) codes, which are not failures.

userMessageKey.description: Translation key reference rendered as the messages argument on the emitted D2Result factory (e.g. TK.Common.Errors.NOT_FOUND). Written as the C# symbol-path string — the .NET emitter uses it verbatim in the factory body; the TS emitter passes it to tk(...). The generator enforces existence by inverse-transforming the symbol path to the snake_case key (TK.Common.Errors.NOT_FOUND -> common_errors_NOT_FOUND) and asserting membership in contracts/messages/en-US.json. The code and the userMessageKey constant may legitimately differ (e.g. UNHANDLED_EXCEPTION -> TK.Common.Errors.UNKNOWN; RATE_LIMITED -> TK.Common.Errors.TOO_MANY_REQUESTS).

factoryName.description: PascalCase symbol for the emitted factory method (e.g. NotFound, ValidationFailed, TooManyRequests). In the generic catalog the factory is a constructing member on D2Result / D2Result<TData>. The .NET emitter uses it as-is; the TS emitter lowercases the first character to camelCase (notFound). May differ from code (e.g. code RATE_LIMITED -> factoryName TooManyRequests). Must be unique across the spec.

factoryShape.description: Closed enum that drives the generated factory's signature variant. standard: the one universal error-factory shape (messages?, inputErrors?, errorCode?, category?, traceId?) — ALL optional. Every error factory can stamp a more specific code + category on the base status and optionally carry inputErrors; the overrides are how callers / delegating domain factories specialize the base factory. none: no factory generated for this code; only the constant + boolean are emitted — used by codes with no current constructing factory (the data-carrying SOME_FOUND / PARTIAL_SUCCESS 206/207 factories stay hand-rolled, plus IDEMPOTENCY_IN_FLIGHT / the serialization codes).

doc.description: XML <summary> text rendered on the emitted constant + factory (.NET) and JSDoc text on the emitted TS constant + factory.

deprecated.description: When true, the entry is deprecated: the generated C# constant + factory carry [Obsolete] and the TS constant + factory carry @deprecated. Additive — the entry is NEVER deleted. Deleting a deprecated (or non-deprecated) entry is a breaking change the JSON-diff gate fails.

deprecatedReason.description: Plain dev-facing English explaining why the code is deprecated; rendered into the [Obsolete("...")] message, the @deprecated JSDoc, and the changelog entry. NOT a TK key (developer-facing, not user-facing).

replacedBy.description: Wire code of the successor entry (e.g. RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND). Must reference a real, non-deprecated entry in the merged registry. Omit for pure retirement with no replacement.

sunset.description: ISO-8601 date for the future RFC 8594 Sunset response header. Edge-runtime-deferred: carried on the contract now, consumed by the Edge response middleware when it exists. Inert until then.
